metadata.notes: Standard ACOL: weak 1NT (12-14), 4-card majors, Acol 2s, weak 2s in the majors, Stayman, Jacoby transfers, standard Blackwood, 2nd/4th leads, standard attitude signals.

leads.method: Top of sequence (A from AK), 4th highest from honour suits, top of doubleton
4th highest from longest and strongest; top of internal sequence (e.g., J from KJ10x)
2nd highest from bad suits (no honour), 4th highest from any suit headed by an honour

signals.method: Standard attitude: high card encourages, low card discourages
Standard count: high-low shows even, low-high shows odd
signals.context: Count signal when attitude is known or declarer leads

signals.method: High card = preference for higher suit, low card = lower suit
signals.context: Suit preference when giving ruff or in obvious situations

discards.method: Standard attitude: high card in a suit encourages that suit
Standard count when attitude already shown

nt-responses.name: 1NT Responses
nt-responses.description: Responses to 1NT opening (12-14 HCP): Stayman, Jacoby transfers, invitational and game bids

meaning.description: Stayman — asks for 4-card major, 8+ HCP or weak with both majors
4 spades, 4 hearts — pick a major, game forcing
Game values, no heart fit — to play 3NT
Game values, no spade fit — to play 3NT
Jacoby transfer to hearts — 5+ hearts, any strength
Exactly 5 hearts, offers choice of game, 10+ HCP
Jacoby transfer to spades — 5+ spades, any strength
Exactly 5 spades, offers choice of game, 10+ HCP
Game forcing, 5+ clubs, slam interest or hand unsuitable for NT
No major stopper, minimum, club fit query
Stoppers in all suits, no particular club fit

blackwood.name: Standard Blackwood
blackwood.description: 4NT asks for aces. 5C=0/4, 5D=1, 5H=2, 5S=3. Follow-up 5NT asks for kings.

major-raises.name: Major Suit Raises (ACOL)
major-raises.description: Raises after 1H or 1S opening. 4-card majors. Simple raise = 6-9 HCP 4+ trumps. Limit raise via 2NT or direct 3M = 10-12. Game raise = 13+.

meaning.description: Good hand, game try accepted or own decision
To play — 4+ trumps, 8-10 HCP (pre-emptive), or weaker hand with distributional values
Minimum opener, rebid of major — poor hand
Singleton or second suit below the major
Good trumps, nothing extra to show

one-nt-rebid.name: Opener Rebids after 1NT (15-16 balanced)
one-nt-rebid.description: When opener rebids 1NT (15-16) after a one-level response

meaning.description: Checkback Stayman — asks for 3-card support or 4-card major
3+ card support for hearts (if hearts response) or 4 hearts (if spades response)
4+ clubs, or balanced 12-14 (may be short club), 12-19 HCP
4+ diamonds, 6+ HCP — forcing for one round
4+ hearts, 6+ HCP — forcing for one round
Minimum balanced, 15-16 HCP, no heart fit
4+ spades, 6+ HCP — forcing for one round
Minimum balanced, 15-16 HCP, no spade fit
Game try in NT — good 17+ HCP
4+ hearts, 6+ HCP — forcing for one round
4+ spades, 6+ HCP — forcing for one round
10+ HCP, 5+ clubs — forcing for one round
6-9 HCP, denies 4+ spades (not forcing in ACOL)
10+ HCP, 4+ clubs, forcing for one round
Game try in NT, balanced 18-19 HCP
10+ HCP, 4+ diamonds, forcing for one round
To play — 4+ hearts, 8-10 HCP or distributional, pre-emptive
Invitational, balanced 11-12 HCP, no heart fit
To play, 13+ HCP balanced, no heart fit
6-9 HCP, may have 4-card minor (not forcing in ACOL)
10+ HCP, 4+ clubs, forcing for one round
10+ HCP, 4+ diamonds, forcing for one round
10+ HCP, 5+ hearts, forcing for one round
To play — 4+ spades, 8-10 HCP or distributional, pre-emptive
Balanced: no 5-card major, no singleton/void, 12-14 HCP
ACOL Two Clubs — strong, artificial, forcing: 22+ balanced or 8+ tricks any suit
Waiting / negative — fewer than 8 HCP, or balanced waiting
Positive — 5+ hearts, 8+ HCP, two of top three honours
Positive — 5+ spades, 8+ HCP, two of top three honours

meaningOverride.description: Weak two — 6+ diamonds, wider range in 3rd seat

meaning.description: Ogust — asks about hand quality and suit quality
Minimum hand, good suit (two of top three)

meaningOverride.description: Weak two — 6+ hearts, wider range in 3rd seat

meaning.description: Ogust — asks about hand quality and suit quality

meaningOverride.description: Weak two — 6+ spades, wider range in 3rd seat

meaning.description: Ogust — asks about hand quality and suit quality
Puppet Stayman — asks for 5-card major or 4-card major
Pass or correct — find the solid suit
